promotion_criteria: validation relative annualized return exceeds the linear baseline
cost stress result remains better than the baseline
TopK=8/10/12 remains stable rather than collapsing
year-by-year performance is not driven mainly by one or two years
average cash weight is not improved mainly by holding more cash
turnover does not become materially uncontrolled
RankIC and RankICIR do not materially deteriorate
results are not mainly explained by single-industry, microcap, or low-liquidity concentration
failure_criteria: validation does not improve versus the baseline
cost stress fails after transaction cost pressure
apparent improvement is mainly caused by higher turnover
apparent improvement is mainly caused by high cash weight or low invested exposure
TopK sensitivity collapses under small perturbations
results are concentrated in only a small number of years
effect can only be maintained by repeatedly changing features or parameters
